text: Hello everyone. Today we will show you a minimally invasive abdominoperineal excision of a rectal tumor in a sixty five year old lady which was involving the external sphincter. Neoadjuvant chemoradiation was given prior to surgery.

The surgeon incises the visceral peritoneum over the aorta, continuing cranially to the origin of the inferior mesenteric artery, which is divided between clips. Using an advanced bipolar device, the sigmoid vein is fused and divided. Lateral dissection mobilizes the sigmoid and lower left colon, revealing the left ureter alongside the ovarian vessels. A complete mesorectal dissection is performed around the rectum, preserving the pelvic nerves.

The surgeon completes the posterior, lateral, and anterior mobilization, leaving the anorectal junction attached to the lowermost part of the posterior vaginal wall. The procedure continues via the perineum, where the anus is separated from the ischorectal tissue, and a cuff of the levator ani is excised. The rectum is delivered through the perineal wound after dividing the anterior attachment to the vagina. The specimen is resected, and the proximal colonic stump is exteriorized to form an end colostomy.
